description_long: This helicopter features a modern, military-inspired design with a dual-rotor tiltrotor configuration, reminiscent of the Bell Boeing V-22 Osprey. The fuselage is angular and streamlined, painted in a muted greenish-gray with subtle panel lines and a robust, armored cockpit. The cockpit has large, multi-pane windows with a slight blue-green tint, providing wide visibility for the crew. The helicopter is mounted on a circular base with a red and yellow star emblem, suggesting it may be used for display or as a model. The tail section includes twin vertical stabilizers with small ducted fans, and the landing gear consists of sturdy, metallic skids. The overall appearance is utilitarian and tactical, suitable for urban or military scenarios.
description: A modern, dual-rotor military helicopter with a streamlined green-gray body, large tinted cockpit windows, and robust landing skids, mounted on a circular base with a star emblem.
description_view_0: Front view shows the angular cockpit, large tinted windows, and both main rotors clearly, with the star-emblazoned base prominent.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the tiltrotor mechanism, cockpit profile, and the twin vertical stabilizers at the tail.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the twin vertical stabilizers, tail fans, and the full width of the rotors, with the base mostly obscured.
description_view_3: Opposite side view reveals the cockpit side, rotor assembly, and the structure of the landing skids.
attribute_rotor_blades: There are two main tiltrotor assemblies, each with three long, straight blades made of composite material, colored gray with black tips and no visible markings.
attribute_tail_rotor: The tail features two small ducted fans, each with three blades, oriented vertically within protective housings, colored light gray with no visible markings.
attribute_landing_skids: The helicopter has two robust, metallic landing skids, painted gray, with visible crossbars and support struts for stability.
attribute_cockpit_windows: The cockpit has four large, angular windows: one front-facing, two side panels, and one overhead, all tinted blue-green with dark framing and no visible markings.
